data.title: Tracking Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Portfolio - Q4 2025 Update
data.description:
This article is part of a series that provides an ongoing analysis of the changes made to Berkshire Hathaway’s 13F stock portfolio on a quarterly basis. It is based on Warren Buffett’s regulatory 13F Form filed on 02/17/2026. Please visit our Tracking 10 Years Of Berkshire Hathaway's Investment Portfolio article series for an idea of how his holdings have progressed over the years and our previous update for the moves during Q3 2025.

During Q4 2025, Berkshire Hathaway’s (BRK.A) (BRK.B) 13F stock portfolio value increased from ~$267B to ~$274B. The top five positions account for ~70% of the portfolio: Apple Inc., American Express, Bank of America, Coca-Cola, and Chevron. There are 40 individual stock positions, many of which are small compared to the overall size of the portfolio.

Warren Buffett’s writings (PDFs) are a treasure trove of information and are a very good source for anyone starting out on individual investing. So are the classics “The Intelligent Investor” and “Security Analysis.”

Note 1: It was disclosed in August 2020 that Berkshire had built ~5% stakes in five Japanese trading businesses: ITOCHU, Marubeni, Mitsubishi, Mitsui, and Sumitomo. The stakes increased to over 10% each as of Q1 2025. The 2021 AR had the following regarding the initial purchases in three of them: 5.6% ownership stake in ITOCHU Corporation (ITOCF) at a cost basis of $4.70, 5.5% ownership stake in Mitsubishi Corporation (MSBHF) at a cost basis of $8.57, and 5.7% ownership stake in Mitsui & Co. (MITSF) at a cost basis of $8.65. ITOCF, MSBHF, and MITSF currently trade at ~$15, ~$32, and ~$36, respectively. The cost basis is adjusted to reflect stock splits.

Note 2: Berkshire Hathaway fully exited their stake in BYD Company (BYDDY) by September 2025. Their ownership stake was ~20% as of Q3 2022. The stake fell below the 5% ownership threshold during Q2 2024.

New Stakes:

New York Times (NYT): NYT is 0.13% of the portfolio position established this quarter at prices between ~$54 and ~$71. The stock currently trades above that range at $74.03.

Stake Increases:

Chevron Corporation (CVX): CVX is a large (top five) 7.24% of the portfolio position purchased during Q3 2020 at prices between ~$72 and ~$91. Q1 2021 saw a ~50% selling at prices between ~$85 and ~$112, while in Q3 2021 there was a ~25% stake increase at prices between ~$94 and ~$106. That was followed with another one-third increase in the next quarter at prices between ~$102 and ~$119. Q1 2022 saw a whopping ~315% stake increase at prices between ~$119 and ~$171. The three quarters through Q3 2023 saw one-third selling at prices between ~$150 and ~$188. There was a ~14% stake increase during Q4 2023 at prices between ~$142 and ~$169. The stock currently trades at ~$181. H1 2024 saw a ~6% trimming, while during Q2 2025 there was a ~3% increase. This quarter also saw a ~7% stake increase.

Note 1: Berkshire has a ~6.2% ownership stake in the business.

Note 2: Berkshire avoided disclosing this stake in the Q3 2020 13F filing by making use of the section 13(f) Confidential Treatment Requests. An amendment filed later disclosed the activity.

Chubb Limited (CB): CB is 3.90% of the portfolio position purchased during H2 2023 at prices between ~$188 and ~$230. The next quarter saw a ~30% stake increase at prices between ~$225 and ~$259. That was followed by a ~16% further increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$264 and ~$291. This quarter also saw a ~9% further increase at prices between ~$265 and ~$315. The stock currently trades at ~$332. They are building this position.

Note: Berkshire avoided disclosing this stake in the Q3 2023 13F filing by making use of the section 13(f) Confidential Treatment Requests. They have an ~8.5% ownership stake in the business.

Domino’s Pizza (DPZ): DPZ is 0.51% of the portfolio position purchased during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$402 and ~$504. There was an ~87% stake increase during the next quarter at prices between ~$407 and ~$475. Q1 2025 saw a ~10% further increase at prices between ~$397 and ~$501. That was followed by a ~13% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$421 and ~$496. This quarter also saw a ~12% stake increase at prices between ~$393 and ~$442. The stock currently trades below their purchase price ranges at ~$374. They are continuing to build this position.

Note: They have a 9.9% ownership stake in the business.

Lamar Advertising (LAMR): The very small 0.06% LAMR stake was established during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$100 and ~$123, and it is now above that range at ~$133. There was a minor ~3% stake increase during the last quarter and a marginal increase this quarter.

Stake Decreases:

Apple Inc. (AAPL): AAPL is currently the largest 13F portfolio stake by far at ~23%. It was built between Q1 2016 and Q1 2018 at a cost basis of ~$35 per share. The stake was decreased by 13% during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$169 and ~$195. The next quarter saw another ~49% selling at prices between ~$165 and ~$216. That was followed by a ~25% reduction during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$207 and ~$234. Q2 2025 also saw a ~7% trimming at prices between ~$169 and ~$225. That was followed by a ~15% reduction during the last quarter at prices between ~$202 and ~$258. The stock currently trades at ~$264. This quarter also saw a ~4% trimming.

Bank of America (BAC): Berkshire established this large (top three) ~10% of the portfolio position through the exercise of Bank of America warrants. The warrants had a strike price of $7.14. The cost to exercise was $5B, and it was funded using the $5B in 6% preferred stock they held. There was a ~30% stake increase in Q3 2018 at prices between $27.75 and $31.80. Q3 2024 saw ~23% selling at prices between $36.41 and $43.84. That was followed by a ~15% reduction during the next quarter at prices between $39 and $47.51. Q1 2025 also saw a ~7% trimming at prices between ~$39 and ~$48. That was followed by a ~4% trimming during the next quarter at prices between ~$33 and ~$48. The last quarter also saw a ~6% trimming at prices between ~$45 and ~$53. There was a ~9% further reduction this quarter at prices between ~$48.50 and ~$56.50. The stock currently trades at $52.74. They are harvesting gains.

Note: Berkshire’s overall cost basis is ~$14, and ownership stake is at ~6.8%.

DaVita Inc. (DVA): DVA is 1.32% of the portfolio position that was aggressively built over several quarters in the 2012-13 timeframe at prices between $30 and $49. The stock currently trades at ~$150 compared to Berkshire’s overall cost basis of ~$45 per share. Q3 2020 saw ~5% (2M shares) selling at $88 per share. The last four quarters also saw a ~15% trimming.

Note: Berkshire’s ownership stake in DaVita is ~40%.

Constellation Brands (STZ): STZ is 0.65% of the portfolio position purchased during Q4 2024 at prices between ~$218 and ~$255. The next quarter saw the position increase by ~114% at prices between ~$161 and ~$229. That was followed by a ~12% increase during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$159 and ~$197. The stock currently trades at ~$153. There was a minor ~3% trimming this quarter.

Note: They have a ~7.2% ownership stake in the business.

Aon plc (AON): AON is 0.46% of the portfolio position established during Q1 2021 at prices between ~$202 and ~$234. The next quarter saw a ~7% increase at prices between ~$230 and ~$259. There was a ~12% reduction this quarter at prices between ~$324 and ~$370. The stock currently trades at ~$326.

Pool Corporation (POOL): The 0.26% of the portfolio POOL stake was established during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$294 and ~$372. The next quarter saw a ~48% stake increase at prices between ~$339 and ~$390. That was followed by a ~145% stake increase during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$312 and ~$375. The next quarter saw another ~135% stake increase at prices between ~$282 and ~$327. There was a ~11% trimming this quarter at prices between ~$227 and ~$314. The stock is now at ~$256.

Note: They have an 8.2% ownership stake in the business.

Amazon.com (AMZN): AMZN is 0.19% of the portfolio stake established during Q1 2019 at prices between ~$75 and ~$91. The stake was increased by ~11% in the next quarter at prices between ~$85 and ~$98. This quarter saw a ~77% reduction at prices between ~$211 and ~$259. The stock currently trades at ~$201. They are harvesting gains.

Atlanta Braves (BATRK) and Liberty Latin America (LILA): These positions were reduced during the quarter.

Kept Steady:

American Express (AXP) and Coca-Cola (KO): These two very large stakes were kept steady during the last ~10 years. Buffett has said these positions will be held permanently. Berkshire’s cost basis on AXP and KO are at around $8.49 and $3.25, respectively, and the ownership stakes are at ~20% and ~9.2%, respectively.

Moody's Corporation (MCO): MCO is 4.60% of the 13F portfolio stake. It is a very long-term position, and Buffett’s cost basis is $10.05. The stock currently trades at ~$423. Berkshire controls ~13% of the business.

Occidental Petroleum (OXY): Berkshire made a $10B investment in OXY in April 2019 through 100,000 preferred shares that have a liquidation value of $100,000 per share. Those shares pay an 8% dividend, and the transaction came with warrants to purchase 83.86M shares at $59.62. In Q1 2022, Berkshire purchased ~136.4M shares at prices between ~$31 and ~$59 per share. That was followed with a ~22M share net increase in Q2 2022 at prices between ~$57 and ~$60. The next quarter also saw a ~23% stake increase at prices between ~$57 and ~$75. The three quarters through Q2 2024 also saw a ~15% stake increase. The stock currently trades at $45.94. There was a minor ~3% stake increase during Q4 2024 and a marginal increase during the next quarter.

Note 1: Including warrants, Berkshire owns ~33% of the business (~349M shares). Those warrants expire one year after the preferred shares are fully redeemed. The tenth-year anniversary of preferred issuance from when they can be redeemed at 105% of PAR starts in 2029. 15% of the $10B in preferred was redeemed in 2023 at 110% of PAR (terms of the preferred required OXY to redeem a portion if they returned more capital to shareholders than the threshold specified).

Note 2: The publicly traded warrants under the symbol OXY.WS were initially distributed in June 2020 and allow holders to purchase one share of OXY common stock at $22 per share until August 3, 2027. A temporary offer allowed holders to exercise the warrants at a reduced price of $21.30 until March 31, 2025.

Kraft Heinz Company (KHC): KHC is currently a fairly large position at 2.88% of the portfolio. Kraft Heinz started trading in July 2015, with Berkshire owning just over 325M shares (~26.5% of the business). The stake came about because of two transactions with 3G Capital as partner: a ~$4B net investment in 2013 for half of Heinz and a ~$5B investment for the acquisition of Kraft Foods Group in early 2015. Berkshire’s cost basis on KHC is ~$30 per share compared to the current price of $23.78.

Note: A regulatory filing in January indicates Berkshire’s intention to sell this position.

Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL): GOOGL is 2.04% of the portfolio position purchased during the last quarter at prices between ~$174 and ~$257. The stock currently trades above that range at ~$302.

Kroger Co. (KR): KR is 1.14% of the portfolio position established in Q4 2019 at prices between $24 and $29. The five quarters through Q3 2021 had seen a ~225% stake increase at prices between ~$30 and ~$40. The three quarters through Q3 2022 saw a ~20% selling at prices between ~$44 and ~$52. The stock currently trades at $69.66.

Note: Berkshire’s ownership stake in Kroger is ~7%.

Liberty Live Holdings (FWONK) (LLYVK) (LLYVA): These tracking stocks were first acquired as a result of Liberty Media’s recapitalization in April 2016. Shareholders received 1 share of Liberty SiriusXM Group, 0.25 shares of Liberty Media Group, and 0.1 shares of Liberty Braves Group for each share held. Berkshire held 30M shares of Liberty Media, for which they received the same amount of Liberty SiriusXM Group shares. Since then, there have been several transactions, redistributions, etc. Last August saw another reclassification whereby Liberty SiriusXM common stock was reclassified, and shareholders received 0.25 shares of Liberty Live for each share of Liberty SiriusXM. Also, Liberty Formula One was reclassified, and shareholders received 0.0428 shares of Liberty Live for each share of Liberty Formula One. The net share count increased by ~23% as a result of these transactions. The Liberty SiriusXM stake saw a ~55% stake increase during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$28.50 and ~$31.25. There was a ~18% overall reduction during H1 2025. FWONK and LLYVK currently trade at $86.74 and $94.21, respectively.

Sirius XM Holdings (SIRI): The stake came about as a result of a redistribution transaction by Liberty Media Group. The position was increased by ~315% during Q4 2023 as SIRI traded between ~$41 and ~$57. Q2 2024 saw another ~260% stake increase at prices between ~$25 and ~$38. That was followed by a ~12% stake increase during Q4 2024 at prices between $20.37 and $28.44. It currently trades at $21.02. The last quarter also saw a ~4% stake increase.

Note: In December 2023, Liberty Media announced a plan to simplify the structure - Liberty SiriusXM to combine with Sirius XM to form a new entity. The consolidation ratios were 0.8375 of new SIRI for each LSXMK/LSXMA and 0.1 of new SIRI for each SIRI held. The transaction closed during Q1 2025. The prices quoted above are adjusted based on the conversion ratio.

VeriSign Inc. (VRSN): VRSN was first purchased in Q4 2012 at prices between $34 and $49.50. The position was more than doubled in Q1 2013 at prices between $38 and $48. The buying continued till Q2 2014 at prices up to $63. The last quarter saw one-third selling at prices between ~$261 and ~$311. The stock currently trades at ~$216, and the position is at 0.80% of the portfolio (~10% of the business).

Capital One Financial (COF): The 0.63% of the portfolio stake in COF was purchased during Q1 2023 at prices between ~$90 and ~$122. There was a ~25% stake increase in the next quarter at prices between ~$86 and ~$114. Q2 2024 saw a ~21% reduction at prices between ~$133 and ~$148. The next quarter also saw a ~7% trimming at prices between ~$131 and ~$153. That was followed by an ~18% selling during Q4 2024 at prices between ~$145 and ~$192. The stock currently trades at ~$207.

UnitedHealth Group (UNH): UNH is 0.61% of the portfolio position purchased during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$249 and ~$606. The stock currently trades at ~$289.

Ally Financial (ALLY): The 0.48% ALLY stake saw a whopping ~235% increase during Q2 2022 at prices between ~$32 and ~$53. The stock currently trades at $41.29.

Note: Berkshire has a ~9.6% ownership stake in Ally Financial.

Louisiana-Pacific (LPX): The 0.17% LPX position was established during Q4 2022 at prices between ~$48.40 and ~$65. There was a ~22% stake increase in the next quarter at prices between ~$51 and ~$66. The stock is now at $88.86. There was a ~6% trimming during Q1 2024, and that was followed by a ~10% trimming during the next quarter. Q4 2024 also saw a ~5% trimming.

Note: Berkshire has an ~8% ownership stake in the business.

HEICO Corporation (HEI): The 0.12% HEI stake was purchased during Q2 2024 at prices between ~$188 and ~$229. There was a ~11% stake increase during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$217 and ~$273. That was followed by a similar increase during the next quarter at prices between ~$229 and ~$328. The stock is now at ~$337.

Charter Communications (CHTR): CHTR is 0.08% of the portfolio position. It was established during the last three quarters of 2014 at prices between $118 and $170. In Q2 2015, the position was increased by ~42% at prices between $168 and $193, and that was followed with another ~21% increase the following quarter at prices between $167 and $195. The six quarters through Q4 2018 had seen a combined ~25% selling at prices between $250 and $395, and that was followed with a ~20% reduction in Q1 2019 at prices between $285 and $366. H2 2021 saw another ~25% reduction at prices between ~$605 and ~$821. That was followed by similar selling during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$291 and ~$383. The next quarter also saw another ~30% reduction at prices between ~$315 and ~$408. There was a ~47% selling during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$312 and ~$437. The stock currently trades at ~$237 compared to Berkshire’s cost basis of ~$178.

Allegion plc (ALLE): ALLE is a very small 0.05% of the portfolio stake purchased during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$117 and ~$146. The stock is now well above that range at ~$163.

Diageo plc (DEO), Jefferies Financial Group (JEF), Lennar Corporation (LEN.B), Mastercard Incorporated (MA), NVR, Inc. (NVR), Nucor Corporation (NUE), and Visa Inc. (V): These positions were kept steady this quarter.
